answer:
To determine the primes \( p \) for which the congruence \( 8x \equiv 1 \pmod{p} \) has no solutions, we analyze the existence of a multiplicative inverse of \( 8 \) modulo \( p \).

A solution exists if and only if \( \gcd(8, p) = 1 \), meaning \( p \neq 2 \). Thus, the only prime \( p \) for which no solution exists is \( p = 2 \).

The sum of all such primes is therefore \( \boxed{2} \).
